name: O Carballiño, Maside, and Cea Loop
name_original: Carballiño – vuelta por Maside y Cea
description: A challenging 20.6-mile touring cycling route through Galicia's Ourense region, featuring 1514 feet of climbing and rural scenery.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around O Carballiño traverse a diverse landscape of river valleys, extensive indigenous forests, and varied topography. The region, nestled in the province of Ourense, Galicia, features paths that wind alongside the Arenteiro and Barbantiño rivers, offering both moderate and challenging climbs. Cyclists can explore routes through rural scenery and forested areas, with some trails leading to natural features like the Fervenza do Cachón waterfall or cultural sites such as the Monastery of Oseira. The terrain ranges from gentle riverside paths to more demanding ascents through hills and mountainous areas. Best touring cycling routes around O Carballiño The most popular touring cycling route is O Carballiño, Maside, and Cea Loop, a 20.6 miles (33.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete. This route features rural scenery and significant climbing through the Ourense region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Outariz Hot Springs – Millennium Bridge loop from O Carballiño, a difficult 48.8 miles (78.6 km) path. This route primarily uses paved surfaces, connecting hot springs and river views. Local touring cyclists also love the Oseira Monastery – Oseira Fountain loop from O Carballiño, a 33.6 miles (54.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain and past the historic Monastery of Oseira, often completed in about 4 hours 38 minutes. Touring cycling around O Carballiño is defined by river valleys, indigenous forests, and varied topography including hills and mountainous areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in O Carballiño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore O Carballiño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around O Carballiño?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around O Carballiño, offering a diverse range of experiences. The network includes 7 moderate routes and 16 more challenging options, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in O Carballiño?
A: The terrain around O Carballiño is quite varied, featuring river valleys, extensive indigenous forests, and hills that can lead to significant elevation gains. You'll find paths winding alongside rivers like the Arenteiro and Barbantiño, as well as routes through rural scenery and more mountainous areas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in O Carballiño?
A: Yes, many routes in O Carballiño are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017446434/o-carballino-maside-and-cea-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Carballiño, Maside, and Cea Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, as is the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6952177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs – Millennium Bridge loop from O Carballiño&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while touring cycling in O Carballiño?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantiño Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, explore the lush banks of the Arenteiro River, or enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5058560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Embalse de Albarellos&lt;/a&gt;. The Bouteiro Reservoir also offers beautiful plant life and scenic vistas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. A notable cultural stop is the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Oseira&lt;/a&gt;, a 12th-century Romanesque site that can be visited on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40326470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oseira Monastery – Oseira Fountain loop from O Carballiño&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter medieval bridges, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3850157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sobreira Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I visit any thermal spas or wellness spots during my cycling trip?
A: Yes, O Carballiño is known as a spa town. You can incorporate a visit to thermal springs into your ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantes Thermal Park&lt;/a&gt; is one option, and the Gran Balneario de O Carballiño offers therapeutic waters and relaxing environments with gardens.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, O Carballiño offers several challenging routes with significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6952177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs – Millennium Bridge loop from O Carballiño&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 48.8-mile ride with nearly 1000 meters of ascent. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40326470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oseira Monastery – Oseira Fountain loop from O Carballiño&lt;/a&gt;, covering 33.6 miles with similar elevation.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in O Carballiño?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 8 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from serene river valleys to challenging climbs through forests, and the opportunity to combine cycling with visits to historical sites and natural wonders.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by vineyards?
A: Yes, in the southern parts of the region, some routes will take you through areas featuring peculiar terraced vineyards that produce Ribeiro wines. These offer a unique blend of natural beauty and cultural interest, adding to the scenic variety of your ride.

Q: What are some good touring cycling routes for exploring reservoirs or rivers?
A: You can explore the Bouteiro Reservoir on the Arenteiro river, known for its beautiful plant life and panoramic views. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38185962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrelo Reservoir – San Clodio Medieval Bridge loop from O Carballiño&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic ride around the Castrelo Reservoir.

Q: Can I find routes that feature medieval bridges?
A: Yes, the region is home to several historical bridges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3850157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sobreira Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is a notable landmark you might encounter. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19885069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sobreira Medieval Bridge – Millennium Bridge loop from Balneario das Caldas de Partovia&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights this feature.
tips: Area of recreational activities on the outskirts of O Carballiño to the shores of the Arenteiro river.

A small rural town between O Carballiño and Ourense. One notable building is the town's Maside Church of St. Thomas. There are plenty of water fountains to fill your bottles and cool off on a warm day.
A small rural town on the Camino route between Ourense and Santiago de Compostela. You can carb-load by eating the bread the town is most notable for. There is a water fountain in the main square.

Temple located in Carballiño, in Ourense, built in the mid-20th century mixing Romanesque and Gothic styles. Directed by the architect Antonio Palacios, the church stands out for its sculptural exterior influenced by Gaudí's architecture.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult touring bicycle ride on Komoot, requiring very good fitness due to its substantial climbs and descents. While mostly paved, some segments may be challenging, and cyclists should be prepared for significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Carballiño – return via Maside and Cea route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, making it suitable for touring bicycles. However, given its 'difficult' rating, expect significant ascents and descents. While generally well-maintained, some sections might be unpaved or more challenging, potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable spots. In Carballiño, you can see the impressive Church of the Veracruz of O Carballiño and enjoy the Río Arenteiro Riverside Walk. As you pass through Maside, look for the historic Church of San Tomé de Maside. In Cea, you'll find the Clock Tower and Main Square of Cea.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation changes, this route is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It requires a good level of physical fitness and cycling experience to comfortably complete the 33.1 kilometers and 460 meters of ascent.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: The Ourense region experiences warm summers and mild winters. Spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with moderate temperatures and less chance of extreme weather, making them ideal for tackling this challenging route.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Carballiño – return via Maside and Cea cycling route?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on this route, it's primarily a cycling trail. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is well-behaved, accustomed to cycling environments, and kept on a leash, especially when passing through towns or near other people. Always carry waste bags and water for your pet.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route in Carballiño?
faqs.answer: Carballiño, as a larger town, offers various parking options. You can typically find public parking areas near the town center or close to the Arenteiro river, which is a good starting point for the route. Look for designated parking zones or street parking that allows for longer stays.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this cycling trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Carballiño – return via Maside and Cea route. It uses public roads and paths through the Galician countryside.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with a few other official trails. You will find sections that overlap with the Camiño Xabrés (Oeste), Mtb Camiño Xabrés (Oeste), Camiño Natural dos Arrieiros, and the Senda do Arenteiro.

faqs.question: Are there places to refill water or find refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, Maside, a small rural town along the route, is known to have convenient water fountains where you can refill your bottles. Both Carballiño and Cea, as towns, will also offer cafes, shops, and other amenities for refreshments and supplies.

faqs.question: What natural features can I expect to see on this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the rural landscapes of the Ourense region. You'll experience the scenic Arenteiro river, which flows through lush greenery, and pass by old mills. The broader area also features natural attractions like the Barbantiño River waterfall (Fervenza do Cachón), and the sections near Cea offer glimpses of untouched nature and mountain stretches.
